operating_state: Netflix generated $9.37 billion in streaming revenues for Q1 2024, representing 15% growth driven by 14% increase in average paying memberships to 264.9 million and regional price increases. Global paid net membership additions reached 9.3 million with broad strength across all regions. Average monthly revenue per paying membership increased 1% to $11.79, with constant currency growth of 4% excluding foreign exchange headwinds.
cost_structure: Operating margin expanded 700 basis points to 28% as revenue growth outpaced expense increases. Content amortization totaled $3.67 billion with produced content representing half of total amortization. Marketing expenses increased driven by content release timing while technology and development costs rose modestly.
material_events: Netflix repurchased $2.0 billion of common stock during the quarter, acquiring 3.6 million shares under its $15 billion authorization program with $6.4 billion remaining available. The company repaid $400 million of maturing senior notes in March 2024 and entered a new five-year $3 billion unsecured revolving credit facility in April 2024, replacing the previous $1 billion facility.
macro_exposures: Foreign currency translation adjustments resulted in $73 million negative impact on comprehensive income despite exchange gains from euro-denominated debt remeasurement. The company expanded its cash flow hedging program with $9.7 billion notional amount of forward contracts to manage foreign exchange risk on forecasted revenue and content transactions. Brazilian tax authorities continue assessments with potential exposure estimated at approximately $300 million.
forward_outlook: Management maintains focus on global business growth within operating margin targets while improving member experience through content offerings. Content obligations increased to $24.2 billion with $17.4 billion not yet recognized on balance sheet, reflecting ongoing investment in original programming. The company anticipates potential additional debt capital raises for refinancing upcoming maturities.
uncertainty_profile: Content obligations include agreements for unknown future titles where quantity and fees remain undetermined, with additional unknown commitments estimated at $1-4 billion over three years. The company faces ongoing non-income tax audits across various jurisdictions and maintains exposure to foreign exchange fluctuations with potential $437 million adverse impact from 10% currency movement after hedging effects.
